title: BitBuzz
description: A global technology and innovation platform serving 23,500+ users across 50+ countries with coverage of technology, innovation, science, and space—without politics, sensationalism, or bias.
story: BitBuzz began as a simple idea when I was in Grade 9: create a place where people could discover technology, innovation, startups, science, and space without being overwhelmed by politics, negativity, or clickbait. What started as a small passion project gradually evolved into a global platform reaching more than 23,500 users across 50+ countries. Over the years, BitBuzz expanded far beyond a website. It became an ecosystem of tools, experiments, community initiatives, newsletters, events, and student-led projects. As the platform grew, I assembled and led an international team of student contributors who helped operate, improve, and expand BitBuzz. More than any single project I've built, BitBuzz taught me long-term execution, leadership, product development, community building, and the reality of growing something over multiple years. It remains the project I have worked on for the longest and continues to evolve today.
impact: Reached 23,500+ users worldwide
Audience spanning 50+ countries
Built and maintained over 4+ years
Started in Grade 9 and continues today
Established and led a global team of student contributors
Created an ecosystem of products, tools, newsletters, and community initiatives
Focused on unbiased coverage of technology, innovation, science, and space

title: Flag It
description: A community-driven cyber safety platform designed to help people report scams, fraud, and suspicious activity while raising awareness about cybercrime.
story: Flag It started from frustration. I kept seeing people lose money, accounts, and trust to online scams that could have been avoided with better awareness. Rather than just talking about the problem, I decided to build something around it. The platform allows people to anonymously report scams and suspicious activity while helping spread cyber safety awareness. This became one of my favorite projects because it wasn't just another website—it was built around protecting people. The response was far stronger than expected and helped drive significant attention to BitBuzz.
impact: Built to increase cybercrime awareness
Anonymous and free reporting system
Focused on scam and fraud prevention
Generated significant community engagement
Contributed to 10,000+ additional site visits
One of my favorite projects to build

title: Flag It Guardian for Hack Club
description: A Slack bot deployed on Hack Club Nest that detects fraud-related discussions and guides users to report scams through Flag It.
story: Flag It Guardian was built as an extension of Flag It, a cyber safety initiative by BitBuzz focused on helping people report scams, fraud, phishing, and online threats. While building Flag It, I realized that many victims never actively search for reporting resources, even after discussing scams publicly. To solve this, I built Flag It Guardian for the Hack Club Slack workspace. The bot automatically detects fraud-related discussions and provides users with a direct path to report incidents through Flag It. The project was deployed on Hack Club Nest and configured to run 24/7 using PM2, making it one of my first fully deployed cloud-hosted automation projects. Beyond cybersecurity awareness, the project taught me how to work with Slack APIs, cloud infrastructure, deployment pipelines, environment management, and production services.
impact: Deployed to Hack Club Nest
Integrated into Hack Club Slack
Provides 24/7 scam awareness messaging
Automatically detects fraud-related discussions
Directs users to Flag It reporting platform
Built as an extension of the Flag It cyber safety initiative

title: Opportunity Feed
description: A curated feed of hackathons, fellowships, competitions, bounties, internships, and student opportunities from around the world.
story: Opportunity Feed was built around a simple idea: students miss great opportunities because they never hear about them. The platform aggregates hackathons, fests, fellowships, competitions, bounties, and other opportunities into a single feed. Beyond helping students discover opportunities, it also became a useful tool for growing the BitBuzz community and building what eventually became the BitBuzz Squad—a global team of student builders, contributors, and volunteers.
impact: Aggregated opportunities from multiple sources
Helped students discover hackathons and fellowships
Used to recruit members for the BitBuzz Squad
Supported community growth and engagement
Created a central hub for student opportunities

title: Sketchyy
description: A dynamic scam shield and utility platform for travelers, enabling users to track live local crime/scam data, view real-time weather maps, and navigate safely with an interactive scroll-reactive compass.
story: Sketchyy was built to solve a real-world problem for travelers: walking into unfamiliar places completely blind to local scams and high-crime areas. Acting as a digital 'scam shield,' it aggregates safety alerts, local weather data, and live map views into one sober, highly accessible dashboard. To make the interface feel alive and premium, I engineered a minimalist, custom CSS compass widget that spins dynamically as the user scrolls down the page, acting as an anchor for fellow travelers.
impact: Approved for the Hack Club Off-Track Program
Secured a $60 travel grant from Hack Club for a real-world trip to Mussoorie
Tracked 5.15 hours of highly focused development time through Hackatime
Integrated live weather, map systems, and local scam prevention databases
Designed a custom, hardware-independent scroll-responsive UI compass widget

title: Buzz Letter
description: The official digital newsletter platform of my school, serving over 4,000 active readers and showcasing student stories, achievements, and events.
story: Built during the Summer Vacation of 2026, Buzz Letter became the official digital newsletter platform for my school, serving a community of over 4,000 active readers. As the School Captain (ICT) for the 2025–26 session, I led the initiative from concept to launch, designing both the platform and its public-facing website. The project was one of the major objectives of my term and extended far beyond coding. I coordinated with student council members, collaborated closely with the Literature Team, and helped establish a workflow for creating, reviewing, and publishing content. Through this experience, I learned valuable lessons in leadership, project management, delegation, and working with teams toward a shared goal. What began as a technical project evolved into a school-wide publishing platform that gave students a voice, improved communication, and created a lasting digital record of school events and achievements.
impact: Official school newsletter platform
Served 4,000+ active readers
Led as School Captain (ICT) 2025–26
Managed collaboration between Council and Literature teams
Created a sustainable publishing workflow
One of the major objectives completed during my term

title: KEEB Rewards Store
description: The frontend shop interface for Hack Club's KEEB hardware-keyboard YSWS program, built to showcase and distribute custom keyboard rewards.
story: I contributed to Hack Club's ecosystem by building the frontend for the KEEB Rewards Store, a dedicated shop for the custom hardware-keyboard YSWS (You Ship We Ship) program. My work focused on creating an intuitive, clean interface for participants to browse and claim their keyboard gear. This project was built as an open-source contribution under the Fix Hack Club initiative, which allowed me to dive deeper into component-driven development and fine-tune the user experience for the community.
impact: Built the frontend interface for a major Hack Club hardware program
Received a $40 grant as part of the Fix Hack Club initiative
Contributed directly to the open-source Hack Club ecosystem

title: My GitHub Profile
description: The workshop behind the scrapbook. Open-source contributions, experiments, projects, and everything currently in progress.
story: I spent a significant amount of time crafting my GitHub profile because it's where most of the actual building happens. Every repository, contribution, experiment, and project starts here before making its way into the scrapbook.
impact: Primary hub for my code and open-source work
Home to personal projects and experiments
Tracks my development journey through repositories and contributions
Acts as the foundation behind most scrapbook entries

title: Chanakya AI
description: An AI Research & Fact-Checking Assistant built for BitBuzz, inspired by Chanakya's principles of strategy and wisdom. Designed to provide reliable answers while remaining highly efficient through multiple AI fallback systems.
story: Built for BitBuzz as a practical AI assistant focused on reliability and accessibility. The biggest challenge wasn't creating another chatbot—it was making sure it remained available even when individual AI providers failed or ran out of credits. To solve this, I implemented multiple AI API fallbacks, allowing the system to switch providers seamlessly. The result is a fact-checking and research assistant that stays online, serves users for free, and remains surprisingly resilient.
impact: Built for BitBuzz
Multiple AI provider fallback system
Designed to remain available even during API failures
Free for users through efficient resource management
Web search support integrated
Future plans include image generation and image search

title: Delta
description: A kid-friendly chatbot built using Code.org App Lab to make learning interactive and engaging.
story: Built in Grade 8 for a school exhibition, Delta was one of my earliest AI-related projects. The goal was to create a simple and engaging chatbot that could interact with users and make learning more fun. Looking back, what makes this project special is that it was created before ChatGPT and modern AI assistants became widely known, making it one of my first experiments with conversational technology.
impact: Built for a school exhibition
Created in Grade 8
One of my earliest AI-related projects
Built before ChatGPT became mainstream
Introduced students to conversational technology

title: Space Flappy Bird
description: A space-themed version of the classic Flappy Bird built for fun using Code.org.
story: One of my early game development experiments. I wanted to recreate the addictive gameplay of Flappy Bird while giving it a space-themed twist. The project was built primarily for fun and as an opportunity to learn more about game mechanics, collision systems, and player interaction.
impact: Game development experiment
Built using Code.org
Learned game logic and collision detection
One of my earliest programming projects

title: The Vote Master
description: A live voting platform where anyone can launch a poll in seconds. Votes remain open for one hour before results are permanently archived in the Hall of Fame.
story: Built for a BitBuzz event, The Vote Master was designed to make live community voting simple, fast, and engaging. Users could create polls instantly, collect votes in real time, and automatically archive the final results. The project was deployed for a real event and successfully handled more than 200 votes, making it one of my first projects used by a larger audience.
impact: Used during a live event
Collected over 200 votes
Real-world deployment
Automated Hall of Fame system
Designed for fast community engagement

title: Weather Intel
description: A weather intelligence dashboard providing live weather conditions, AQI data, radar visualization, and safety analysis through multiple connected APIs.
story: Weather Intel was one of my first projects built around external APIs. Originally developed as a feature for BitBuzz, it combined multiple weather and environmental data sources into a single dashboard. The project introduced me to working with real-time data, API integration, error handling, and building useful tools powered by live information rather than static content.
impact: My first major API-powered project
Integrated multiple external APIs
Added as a feature to BitBuzz
Provides live weather and AQI data
Includes radar visualization and safety analysis

title: The Summer SchoolWire 2026
description: A public summer challenge where students shared what they were building, reading, learning, and creating during their vacation.
story: The Summer SchoolWire 2026 was a community experiment built around a simple idea: make learning and building visible. Students could post what they were creating during their summer break, represent their school, and compete for prizes funded from grants I had previously earned. While the platform worked as intended, participation was lower than expected. Looking back, the biggest lesson wasn't technical—it was marketing. Building something people can use is only half the challenge; getting it in front of the right audience is equally important.
impact: Community-building experiment
Sponsored using my own grant money
Supported student creators and builders
Learned valuable lessons about distribution and marketing
Successfully launched and operated as a public event

title: HackaTyper
description: An interactive typing speed tester built custom for the global Hack Club community to test and improve their typing metrics.
story: Built for the global Hack Club community, HackaTyper is an interactive typing speed tester designed to give builders a fun, engaging way to track and test their typing metrics. It brings a clean, developer-focused aesthetic to the classic typing test, making it a perfect fit for the Hack Club ecosystem.
impact: Built natively for the global Hack Club community
Provides real-time interactive typing metrics and analytics
Designed with a sleek, hacker-friendly user interface
